records.ety: [OE. offren, AS. offrian to sacrifice, fr. L. offerre; ob (see OB-) + ferre to bear, bring. The English word was influenced by F. offrir to offer, of the same origin. See 1st Bear.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Offered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Offering.]

defs: To present, as an act of worship; to immolate; to sacrifice; to present in prayer or devotion; -- often with up.
quotes: Thou shalt offer every day a bullock for a sin offering for atonement.
A holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices.

defs: To bring to or before; to hold out to; to present for acceptance or rejection; as, to offer a present, or a bribe; to offer one's self in marriage.

To present in words; to proffer; to make a proposal of; to suggest; as, to offer an opinion. With the infinitive as an objective: To make an offer; to declare one's willingness; as, he offered to help me.

quotes: All that offer to defend him.

defs: To bid, as a price, reward, or wages; as, to offer a guinea for a ring; to offer a salary or reward.
To put in opposition to; to manifest in an offensive way; to threaten; as, to offer violence, attack, etc.
To present itself; to be at hand.
quotes: The occasion offers, and the youth complies.

defs: To make an attempt; to make an essay or a trial; -- used with at.
quotes: He would be offering at the shepherd's voice.
I will not offer at that I can not master.

records.ety: [Cf. F. offre, fr. offrir to offer, fr. L. offerre. See Offer, v. t.]

defs: The act of offering, bringing forward, proposing, or bidding; a proffer; a first advance.
That which is offered or brought forward; a proposal to be accepted or rejected; a sum offered; a bid.
quotes: When offers are disdained, and love denied.

defs: Attempt; endeavor; essay; as, he made an offer to catch the ball.
